regime_justification: USDJPY at 159.17, just 83 pips from MOF watch line at 160; Katayama issuing renewed intervention threats; BOJ meeting tomorrow expected to hold rates at 0.75%; JGB 10Y elevated at 2.44%; oil at $96/bbl sustaining imported inflation; vol subdued but carry mechanics intact.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY testing 159.17, MOF rate-checks rumored last week, Katayama rhetoric escalating ('free hand to intervene', 'decisive action'), BOJ meeting tomorrow presents policy fork, JGB yields at multi-week highs, FX vol remains low despite proximity to intervention threshold.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ meeting April 28: 93% no-hike priced, but any hawkish tilt could catalyze unwind; USDJPY 83 pips from 160 watch line; oil $96 feeds imported inflation; rates vol muted but setup fragile.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ market digestion, US-Iran ceasefire extension ends May 11, potential MOF intervention if USDJPY breaches 160, JGB stability vs fiscal concerns, energy prices remain elevated.

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: Strait of Hormuz normalization timeline unclear, BOJ June hike probability 57%, fiscal-monetary tension unresolved, imported inflation sticky above 2% target, carry positioning still elevated.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: If BOJ holds and signals June hike path, some unwind pressure vents; if Hormuz reopens, oil retraces; spread still wide at ~187 bps but vol normalization reduces tail risk.

bottom_line: Stage 2 – Pre-Intervention. USDJPY 159.17, 83 pips from MOF line. BOJ tomorrow: 93% hold priced but policy tilt matters. Katayama threats escalating. Oil $96, JGB 2.44%, spread 187 bps, vol subdued. Grades C/C/C/D: watchful near-term, pressure eases by 4w if policy clarifies and Hormuz normalizes. Next 48 hours are pivot: BOJ rhetoric + USDJPY reaction determine whether we advance to Stage 3 or rotate back to Stage 1.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 159.17, 83 pips from 160 line. Katayama 'free hand' rhetoric escalating. Rate-checks reported last week but no actual intervention Jan 28-present per MOF data. Reserves $1.16T. Catalyst: breach 160 or rapid pace.

April 28 meeting: 93% hold, 7% hike priced. Market expects hold + signal June hike (57% priced). Surprise hawkish tilt (immediate hike or firm June commit) would catalyze unwind. Ueda cited 'upside and downside risks' but Middle East uncertainty weighs.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: JGB 10Y at 2.44%, multi-week high, but well below 2024 peak ~2.5%. Fiscal concerns (Takaichi stimulus) + BOJ normalization path driving. Not yet at regime-shift threshold but trajectory bears watching.
MOVE at 66.97, subdued. Rates vol compressed despite fiscal stress and BOJ meeting tomorrow. No imminent spike signaled but complacency notable.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl at ¥15,226, elevated but below March peak ~¥16,000 (160 USDJPY × $100 WTI). Hormuz closed wk 9, Iran proposal floated but Trump suspended talks. Normalization timeline uncertain; risk skewed to prolonged closure.
5-day change -0.35% (strengthened). Weekly pace subdued. MOF historically reacts to pace not just level. Currently orderly drift, not disorderly move.

narrative.p1: USDJPY opened Monday at 159.17, down 13 bps, hovering 83 pips below the 160 MOF watch line. The pair tested 159.8 last week, triggering fresh Katayama warnings ('free hand to intervene', 'decisive action'), but no actual intervention occurred per latest MOF data through Jan 28. Oil rallied to $96/bbl as Hormuz remains closed (week 9), but an Iranian proposal to reopen the strait circulated Monday, capping gains. JGB 10Y climbed 6 bps over the week to 2.44%, a multi-week high, as markets price BOJ normalization against fiscal expansion concerns. Equity vol compressed: VIX -3.1% to 18.71, MOVE -1.1% to 66.97, and Nikkei closed Friday at a record 59,716, up 0.97%. Tomorrow's BOJ meeting is the pivot: 93% hold priced but any hawkish signal (June hike commitment, upward inflation revision) could catalyze yen strength; conversely, dovish hold citing Middle East uncertainty would reinforce carry. No stage change from Friday's yellow/Stage 2; we remain Pre-Intervention, watching for BOJ tilt or 160 breach.
narrative.p2: Computed anchors: US-JP 10Y spread 187 bps (4.31% - 2.44%), narrowed ~8 bps but still wide and supportive of carry. Carry-to-vol proxy: 187 / 7.72 = 24.2, elevated (carry remains attractive vs realized vol). ¥/bbl crude ¥15,226 (95.66 × 159.17), up 1.2%, sustaining imported inflation despite core CPI print of 1.8% in March. USDJPY 5-day pace -0.35% (yen strengthened modestly), orderly not disorderly; distance from 60d mean roughly neutral. **Domino Stage 2 – Pre-Intervention** fits: USDJPY testing watch line, MOF rhetoric escalating, vol bid muted but risk reversals unavailable to confirm skew, JGB yields elevated, BOJ meeting tomorrow presents binary catalyst. Grades 1w/2w both **C – Watchful**: BOJ meeting and 160 level are immediate trip-wires; if BOJ holds dovish and USDJPY breaches 160, we advance to Stage 3 (Shock/Trigger via intervention or surprise hike). If BOJ signals credible June path and Hormuz normalizes, pressure vents toward Stage 1. **Applied unwind prior to 2w grade**: data ambiguous (vol low, equities strong, but spread wide, oil elevated, MOF threats); without prior would grade D, but proximity to known catalysts (BOJ, 160 line, Hormuz) and setup fragility tip to C. 3w **C**: June BOJ (57% hike priced) and Hormuz timeline keep setup tense. 4w **D**: if policy clarifies and energy normalizes, tail risk recedes; spread still wide but vol mean-reverts and intervention risk fades absent breach. Key asymmetry: downside (unwind) more plausible than upside (renewed carry acceleration) given policy/geopolitical crosscurrents.
narrative.p3: BTC consolidated $77,820, +0.03%, range-bound $77-79k after 9-day ETF inflow streak totaling $2B. Crypto liquidity sympathy remains constructive: risk-on bid intact, VIX sub-19, equities at records. BTC open interest stable, no leverage flush signaled. However, BTC-DXY correlation (~30d) likely positive given dollar strength theme; if yen unwind weakens dollar broadly, BTC could face headwind as carry unwind drains risk appetite. Near-term, BTC tracking equities more than FX; Nikkei record highs and S&P 500 new ATH Friday support crypto bid. For positioning: BTC holding above $76k support signals resilience, but macro overhang (BOJ, Hormuz, Fed meeting April 30) argues for caution on leverage. Unwind scenario (Stage 3→4) would likely see BTC test $73k (21-week MA) as vol spikes and margin calls propagate. Current setup: crypto tracking risk-on, but fragile to policy shock or intervention catalyst. Watch $76k support and Friday's BOJ rhetoric for direction.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 dominated by BOJ meeting April 28 (decision + Ueda presser); market pricing 93% hold but rhetoric on June path and inflation forecast revisions matter. USDJPY 83 pips from 160; any post-BOJ drift toward 160 elevates MOF intervention risk. Week 2 digests BOJ outcome and watches for MOF action if 160 breached. Hormuz timeline uncertain; ceasefire extension expires May 11. Both weeks remain Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention) or advance to Stage 3 if intervention/hike occurs. Grade holds C/C: watchful, catalysts dense.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 to 3 transition depends on BOJ outcome and Hormuz developments. If BOJ holds dovish and USDJPY consolidates below 160, pressure plateaus. If BOJ signals firm June hike (57% priced), yen strength begins unwinding carry positions (move toward Stage 3/4). Hormuz reopening would lower oil, ease ¥/bbl inflation, reduce BOJ urgency. Week 3 grade remains C: June BOJ proximity (June 15-16 meeting) keeps setup fragile; fiscal-monetary tension (Takaichi stimulus vs BOJ normalization) unresolved.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 to 4: if BOJ has clarified June path (hike or hold) and Hormuz normalizes (flows resume), setup de-risks. Spread still wide (187 bps) but vol mean-reverts, intervention risk fades absent 160 breach, and carry positioning adjusts orderly. Grade steps down to D (Unlikely) for 4w: tail risk recedes as binary catalysts resolve. However, if Hormuz remains closed or BOJ signals delay, C persists. Non-monotonic valid: C/C/C/D reflects dense catalysts weeks 1-3 resolving by week 4.

watch_list: BOJ April 28 decision + Ueda press conference: rhetoric on June hike path, inflation/growth forecast revisions, Middle East uncertainty weighting
USDJPY 160.00 level: breach triggers MOF intervention high probability; pace (pips/day) matters as much as level
Hormuz ceasefire developments: Iran proposal circulating; US-Iran talks suspended by Trump; normalization timeline for oil flows
JGB 10Y yield: currently 2.44%; break above 2.50% would signal fiscal stress intensifying, BOJ policy conflict
Oil price trajectory: WTI $96; Hormuz closure week 9; IEA 'biggest supply shock on record'; any spike above $100 escalates ¥/bbl inflation to ~¥16,000
